content.full_text:
April 11, 1975


Mr. Edward Levy
Assistant General Counsel
Association of California
Insurance Companies
11th & L Building, Suite 346
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Levy:

Re: Request for Opinion
No. 75-95

Thank you for your letter of April 1, 1975 requesting an
opinion with respect to the duties of insurance companies under
the Political Reform Act.

Since questions 1 and 2 have been addressed by the
Commission through the adoption of regulations, your opinion
request is denied. Under California Administrative Code
Section 18601, the second transaction is a reportable exchange,
but the first is not. A copy of the regulation is enclosed.

With respect to questions 3 and 4, the Commission has
pending Opinion Request No. 75-75 in which the same questions
will be considered. You will be sent a draft opinion when
one has been prepared, and you are welcome to submit your
views before or after you receive the draft.

Since question 5 does not involve any possible duty of the
Association of California Insurance Companies under the Act, no
opinion will be issued on this question. However, an opinion
will be issued on this subject if the question is presented by
an insurance company in the context of a specific type of policy.

If you wish to discuss the enclosed regulation, or if you
have any further questions, I urge you to contact a member of
the Commission staff.

Sincerely,


Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman

DB:lcjb
Enclosure

ASSOCIATION OF CALIFORNIA INSURANCE COMPANIES
155 MONTGOMERY STREET
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94104

CLAYTON R. JACKSON
GENERAL COUNSEL

DAVID V. OLIVER
GENERAL MANAGER

EDWARD LEVY
ASSISTANT GENERAL COUNSEL

April 1, 1975

Please reply to:
11TH AND I BUILDING, SUITE 345
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814

AREA CODE 916-442-4581

Hon. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman
Fair Political Practice Commission
926 J Building
Sacramento, Calif. 95814

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

We would like to request that the Commission render an
opinion on the following questions:

Assume that an insurance company is an employer of
a lobbyist under Section 86108(a) and must file
the reports required by Section 86109.

Must the insurance company report the following
transactions?:

1 - the sale of an automobile or fire insurance
policy to a designated official or desig-
nated business entity (assume the premium
is in excess of $1,000 and that such policy
premiums are determined from standard rate
manuals used by the insurer);

2 - the sale of a policy such as a commercial
business policy to a designated official or
business entity, the rate for which is in
excess of $1,000 and which is determined
by judgment factors of the underwriter not
using a standard rate manual;

3 - the settlement of a property damage, personal
injury, or workers' compensation insurance
claim for in excess of $1,000, said claim
being made by a designated official against
an insured of the insurance company (this is
the so-called "third party" claim);

Hon. Daniel H. Lowenstein
April 1, 1975
Page 2


4 - the settlement and payment of a claim made
by a designated official or business entity
against his own insurance company based on
the designated official's asserted loss
insured under the policy (the so-called
"first party" claim);

5 - the exercise by an insurer of its right to
repair damaged property of the insured under
a property insurance policy rather than pay
the insured the value of the damaged or des-
troyed property.

Sincerely,

ASSOCIATION OF CALIFORNIA
INSURANCE COMPANIES

[signature]
EDWARD LEVY
Assistant General Counsel

EL:em
